description_long: This skateboard features a standard double-kick deck with a visually striking graphic design on its underside, dominated by a mix of black, white, and blue geometric patterns. The deck appears to be constructed from layered wood, with a natural wood edge visible along the sides. The skateboard is equipped with four orange wheels, likely made of polyurethane, attached to metal trucks that are mounted beneath the deck. The trucks are of a standard style, with no visible branding or unique features. The top of the deck is not fully visible, but the grip tape appears to be patterned or partially cut out, allowing some of the deck's wood to show through. The overall build and design suggest it is intended for street or park use, with a focus on both aesthetics and functionality.
description: A skateboard with a geometric black, white, and blue graphic deck, orange wheels, and natural wood edges. The trucks are standard, and the grip tape appears patterned or partially cut out.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the skateboard, the geometric deck graphic, orange wheels, and natural wood edge.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded nose, central blue stripe, and the symmetry of the deck's graphic design.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the deck's graphic, orange wheels, and wood edge from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail shape, the continuation of the geometric pattern, and the deck's width.
attribute_deck_graphic_design: The deck features a bold geometric design with black, white, and blue zigzag and linear patterns running lengthwise, creating a dynamic and modern visual effect. No text or branding is visible.
attribute_wheel_type: classic street wheels, medium size, solid orange, no visible text or logos.
attribute_truck_style: Standard silver trucks, top-mounted, with a conventional shape and no visible branding or unique features.
attribute_grip_tape_pattern: Patterned or partially cut-out grip tape, allowing some of the natural wood deck to show through; no visible graphics or text.
